eras.name: Mughal Imperial Era
eras.description: A period defined by opulent courtly attire featuring intricate Zardozi embroidery, rich brocades, and Persian-influenced silhouettes. The aesthetic emphasized royalty, using gold threads and precious stones embedded in fabric.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A high-detail historical illustration of a Mughal princess wearing a silk Peshwas with heavy gold Zardozi embroidery, a sheer dupatta with kiran lace, and layered traditional jewelry, set in an ornate marble palace.

eras.name: British Raj Influence
eras.description: Traditional silhouettes began merging with Victorian tailoring. Men increasingly adopted the Sherwani as a formal alternative to Western suits, while women’s garments saw changes in sleeve cuts and lace usage.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A portrait of a 1920s South Asian nobleman wearing a structured black Sherwani with brass buttons and a white churidar, standing in a colonial-era study with teak furniture.

eras.name: The Golden Age of PIA
eras.description: A sophisticated era where Pierre Cardin designed uniforms for Pakistan International Airlines, influencing a generation. The look was sleek, featuring slim-fit kameez and high-fashion hair styling.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A vintage 1960s fashion photograph of a Pakistani woman in a tailored silk kameez with a short hemline and slim trousers, featuring a beehive hairstyle and winged eyeliner.

eras.name: The Zia Era
eras.description: A period of mandated modesty where the dupatta became a compulsory accessory in public life and media. Silhouettes became looser and more conservative, moving away from the fitted styles of the previous decades.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A documentary-style photo of 1980s Pakistani street fashion featuring women in modest, loose-fitting cotton shalwar kameez with large cotton shawls draped over their heads.

eras.name: The Rise of High-End Bridal
eras.description: The birth of the modern Pakistani fashion industry with the establishment of the first fashion schools. Designers like HSY revolutionized bridal wear, turning it into a multi-billion rupee luxury market.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A contemporary high-fashion bridal editorial featuring a model in a deep crimson velvet lehenga encrusted with silver dabka work and crystals, shot in a historic Lahore haveli.

eras.name: The Lawn Craze
eras.description: The democratization of fashion through mass-produced designer lawn (fine cotton). Digital printing technology allowed for complex, vibrant patterns that became the standard summer uniform for all social classes.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A vibrant outdoor lifestyle shot of a woman in a digitally printed floral lawn suit with lace inserts, standing in a lush green garden during a sunny Karachi afternoon.

textiles.name: Lawn Cotton
textiles.description: A high-thread-count, lightweight, and breathable cotton fabric essential for the South Asian heat.
textiles.technique: Fine plain weave with combed yarns

textiles.name: Ajrak
textiles.description: A unique block-printed fabric featuring geometric and floral patterns, usually in indigo and crimson.
textiles.technique: Complex woodblock printing using natural dyes and resist-dyeing

textiles.name: Khaadi Net
textiles.description: A hand-loomed fabric that combines the crispness of net with the texture of cotton or silk.

textiles.name: Jamawar
textiles.description: An intricate brocade fabric often featuring paisley motifs, used for formal wear.
textiles.technique: Jacquard weaving with silk and metallic threads

climate.fashionImplication: Extreme summer temperatures necessitate breathable natural fibers like cotton lawn, while cold winters in the north drive a secondary market for heavy pashmina and wool shawls.

culturalInfluences: Regional Ethnic Identities (Sindhi, Punjabi, Pashtun, Balochi)
